reading1.description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading2.description: Read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading3.description: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading4.description: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

reading5.description: You are going to read an article. For questions 31–36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

reading6.description: You are going to read an article. Six sentences have been removed from the article. Choose from the sentences (A–G) the one which fits each gap. There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

reading7.description: You are going to read an article. For questions 43–52, choose from the sections (A–D). The sections may be chosen more than once.

listening1.description: You will hear people talking in eight different situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

listening2.description: You will hear a recording. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

listening3.description: You will hear five short extracts in which people are talking about something. For questions 19–23, choose from the list (A–H) the opinion or feeling each speaker is expressing.

listening4.description: You will hear part of an interview. For questions 24–30, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

writing1.description: Write your answer in 140–190 words in an appropriate style.

writing2.description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140–190 words in an appropriate style.

speaking1.description: The examiner asks you questions about yourself, your opinions and your experiences. Answer naturally and try to develop your responses.

speaking2.description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

speaking3.description: Discuss the options shown with your partner for about two minutes, then decide together which option would be best.

speaking4.description: The examiner will ask you questions related to the theme from Part 3. Express and develop your opinions with your partner.
